frameworks.name: attentional template / target template
frameworks.one_line: memory representation of the target that guides search and identification

frameworks.name: relational account of template tuning
frameworks.one_line: template shifts away from distractors via relative feature comparison

frameworks.name: optimal account of template tuning
frameworks.one_line: template shifts to maximize target-distractor distinctiveness

core_question: Does a single target template govern both attentional guidance and identity decisions during visual search, or do these stages rely on different template information?
key_assumptions: search involves a look-identify cycle with distinct guidance and decision stages
the target template can be characterized in feature space relative to distractors
linearly separable distractors induce a directional template shift
manipulations: feature relation between target and linearly separable distractors
stage of search measured (initial gaze guidance vs. subsequent identity decision)
key_findings: initial attentional guidance is consistent with a coarse, relational code
subsequent match decisions are consistent with an optimal, distinctiveness-maximizing representation
the two prior accounts (relational vs. optimal) each fit a different stage of search
interpretations: the target template is not a single fixed source of information; precision differs between sensory selection and identity decisions
the relational-vs-optimal debate is resolved by assigning each account to a different stage of the look-identify cycle
